calendar.matters: PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR A WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDATE (ADDED PER ORDER FILED 3/9/26)
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Petitioners Writ Of Administrative Mandate (Cccp Section 1094.5)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Petitioners Writs Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Paul Sanderson Cimino, Esq. Sunney Darling, Esq. 415-982-1300 Bay ARea Legal Aid for Petitioner.
Edmund Wang, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3857 Office of the City Attorney for Respondent.

Hearing is held.

Parties state appearance. Court and Counsel have a discussion on distinctions between Administrative mandamus and Traditional Mandamus.

The Court shares its views. Court comments, admonishes and poses questions; administrative writ and judicial writ are based on different records, not deciding them together, require full briefing on both records, these are like trials.

Mr. Sanderson-Cimino address the court's concerns; seek clarification.
Mr. Wang seeks clarification re admin record and refiling of documents.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER DANIEL PONCE's Motion For Petitioners Writs Of Mandate.

Petitioner Daniel Ponce's Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandamus and Petition for Traditional Mandamus.

Parties are ordered to meet and confer re briefing schedule and include stipulation re admin record. Parties to send proposed order via contestdept302tr@sftc.org by the end of today.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, For the Record (FTR 9:29:00 - 9:59:09) Reported. (302/JMQ)
